title: Demand for apples
questionText: Consider the demand for apples. Suppose that the price of apples falls to $4 per kg. What will happen to the demand for apples?
answers: There will be a contraction in demand
There will be an expansion in demand
There will be an increase in demand
There will be a decrease in demand

title: Demand for lemonade
questionText: Consider the demand for lemonade. Suppose that the price of lemonade increased from $2 per can to $3 per can. What would happen to the demand for lemonade?
answers: There will be a decrease in demand from 60 cans to 40 cans
There will be an increase in demand from 40 cans to 60 cans
There will be a contraction in demand from 60 cans to 40 cans
There will be an expansion in demand from 40 cans to 60 cans

title: Demand for cars
questionText: Consider the demand curve for cars. Suppose that due to strong economic growth there is an increase in income levels amongst the population. What would happen to the demand for cars?
answers: There would be an increase in demand for cars
There would be a decrease in demand for cars
There would be an expansion in the demand for cars
There would be a contraction in the demand for cars

title: Demand for iPads
questionText: Consider the demand for curve for iPads. Suppose Apple announces that the price of iPads will be reduced by 20% in one month’s time. What will happen to the demand for iPads today?
answers: There will be an increase in demand for iPads
There will be a decrease in demand for iPads
There will be an expansion in demand for iPads
There will be a contraction in demand for iPads

title: Demand for iPads
questionText: Consider the same demand curve for iPads. One month has passed and the price of iPads falls by 10%. What happens to the demand for iPads?
answers: There will be an increase in demand for iPads
There will be a decrease in demand for iPads
There will be an expansion in demand for iPads
There will be a contraction in demand for iPads

title: Demand for iPads
questionText: A recession causes rising unemployment and a 5% reduction in the average income of workers in an economy. How would this affect the demand for cinema tickets?
answers: There will be a decrease in demand for cinema tickets by 5%
There will be an unknown decrease in demand for cinema tickets
There will be an increase in the demand for cinema tickets by 5%
There will be an unknown increase in demand for cinema tickets

title: Demand for the children's toy
questionText: A viral marketing campaign on social media for a children’s toy makes it the ‘must have’ Christmas present. How will this affect demand for the toy?
answers: There will be an increase in demand for the toy
There will be a decrease in demand for the toy
There will be an expansion in demand for the toy
There will be a contraction in demand for the toy

answers: There will be an increase in demand for Good A
There will be a decrease in demand for Good A

There will be no change in demand for Good A

title: Demand for Coca-Cola
questionText: Consider the demand curves shown for Coca-Cola. What could explain the shift from D1 to D2?
answers: There has been an increase in the price of Coca-Cola
There has been a decrease in the price of Coca-Cola
There has been an increase in the price of Pepsi
There has been a decrease in the price of Pepsi
